notes: Comprehensive 911 EMS transport provider map for all of Colorado. 205 licensed ground ambulance agencies statewide per CDPHE. This list captures the major known agencies. County-level licensing transitioned to CDPHE state licensing as of July 1, 2024.

agencies.name: Denver Health Paramedics
agencies.notes: 200 medics, 36 ambulances, 100k+ calls/yr. One of busiest in country.

agencies.name: Falck Rocky Mountain
agencies.notes: Exclusive Aurora 911 partner since 2015 (50k+ calls/yr). Boulder contract started Jan 1, 2026 replacing AMR.

agencies.name: South Metro Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: Largest fire-based EMS in Colorado. ~300 sq mi. Merged with Cunningham Fire (2018), Littleton Fire (2019), Parker Fire (2015).

agencies.name: West Metro Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: 130 sq mi, ~300k residents. 17 stations.

agencies.name: North Metro Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: ~60 sq mi, 125k residents. 7 stations, 5 ambulances.

agencies.name: Arvada Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: Transport since 2013. 118 sq mi. 75% of calls are medical. Dispatched by Jeffcom 911.

agencies.name: Thornton Fire Department
agencies.notes: ALS transport. Part of North Area coordinated dispatch with Mountain View and Westminster.

agencies.name: Westminster Fire Department
agencies.notes: 153 personnel, 17k+ calls/yr. Took over patient transport in-house. Part of North Area dispatch.

agencies.name: Federal Heights Fire Department
agencies.notes: Paramedic-level since 2004. 1 ALS ambulance, 2k+ calls/yr. 8 paramedics on staff.

agencies.name: Aurora Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: First response only. Falck handles ambulance transport. AFR paramedics respond alongside Falck.

agencies.name: Mountain View Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: ALS transport since 1997. Part of North Area dispatch. Originally Longmont Rural FPD.

agencies.name: Adams County Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: 4 medic units, each with minimum 1 Paramedic and 1 EMT. ALS transport.

agencies.name: Brighton Fire Rescue District
agencies.notes: Ambulance transport confirmed via billing partner One Billing Solutions.

agencies.name: Castle Rock Fire & Rescue
agencies.notes: Internationally accredited. 66 sq mi. Dispatched by Douglas Regional 911.

agencies.name: AMR Denver
agencies.notes: ~200 employees, ~40k calls/yr. AMR staff embedded in 3 of 6 Longmont fire stations. Lost Boulder city contract to Falck (Jan 2026). Still primary in Longmont.

agencies.name: AMR Boulder County
agencies.notes: Lost City of Boulder contract to Falck Jan 2026. May still serve unincorporated Boulder County areas.

agencies.name: AMR Colorado Springs / El Paso County
agencies.notes: 250 paramedics/EMTs, 62k calls/yr. Contract expired April 2025. City council rejected CSFD enterprise (June 2024). Current status uncertain - may be on extended contract or new RFP.

agencies.name: AMR Pueblo
agencies.notes: 110 paramedics/EMTs, 30k calls/yr. Founded 1994.

agencies.name: AMR Canon City
agencies.notes: 20 paramedics/EMTs, 4,500 calls/yr. Founded 1993.

agencies.name: South Adams County Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: Previously partnered with Northglenn Ambulance (contract ended May 2022). Now partners with Platte Valley Ambulance (since June 2022).

agencies.name: Platte Valley Ambulance Service
coverage: Commerce City (via South Adams County FPD)
agencies.notes: Division of Intermountain Health. South Adams FPD partner since June 2022. Fort Lupton FPD partner since April 2024.

agencies.name: Stadium Medical
agencies.notes: 911 overflow for Denver, Westminster, Thornton. Primary 911 partner with Golden, Pleasant View, Fairmount, Golden Gate fire departments.

agencies.name: Action Care Ambulance
agencies.notes: Operating since 2000. Emergency and non-emergency transport. Exact 911 contract areas need verification.

agencies.name: Apex Paramedics
agencies.notes: ALS and BLS ambulance services. Exact 911 contract areas need verification.

agencies.name: Legacy EMS
agencies.notes: Contracted for 911 transport on military installations. Also provides non-emergent transport in El Paso County.

agencies.name: Highland Rescue Team Ambulance District
coverage: I-70 Morrison Exit to Evergreen Exit
agencies.notes: 33.5 sq mi. 6 paid + 50+ volunteers. 3 ALS ambulances. Founded 1969 as Gilpin County S&R.

agencies.name: Evergreen Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: 120 sq mi, 26k residents. 2 ambulances at Station 2 and 2 at Station 4, staffed 24/7. Merged with Evergreen Ambulance Service in 1986.

agencies.name: Elk Creek Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: Volunteer FD providing fire and EMS. 4 stations, 31 volunteer FF/EMS providers.

agencies.name: Clear Creek EMS
coverage: I-70 Eisenhower Tunnel to Floyd Hill
agencies.notes: 350 sq mi. 17 FT paramedics/EMTs. 2 ALS ambulances 24/7. ~1,800 calls/yr.

agencies.name: Gilpin Ambulance Authority
agencies.notes: 150 sq mi. 3 FT ambulances from 2 stations, staffed 24/7 (EMT+Paramedic). Founded 1969.

agencies.name: Elbert County EMS
agencies.notes: 2 ALS ambulances staffed 24/7 plus on-duty supervisor.

agencies.name: Security Fire Department
agencies.notes: 4 full-time ambulances. ALS and paramedic-level care.

agencies.name: Colorado Springs Fire Department
agencies.notes: 3 squad ambulances for high-volume overflow when all AMR units are busy. Proposed EMS enterprise rejected by council June 2024.

agencies.name: UCHealth EMS
agencies.notes: CAAS accredited. Dispatched by Fort Collins 911 in Larimer County. Signed Greeley/Evans/LaSalle/Platte Valley FPD 911 contract 2021. Bases in Aurora, CO Springs, Fort Collins, LaSalle, Loveland.

agencies.name: Poudre Fire Authority
agencies.notes: 235 sq mi, 189k population. BLS first response; UCHealth EMS handles ALS transport.

agencies.name: Thompson Valley EMS
agencies.notes: 20k+ calls/yr. 15 ambulances, 6 stations, 100+ team members.

agencies.name: Banner Health Paramedics
agencies.parent_org: Global Medical Response (purchased 2022, operates under Banner Health name)
agencies.notes: 30+ year history. Serves estimated 235k citizens in Weld County. Taken over by AMR Aug 2020, then purchased by GMR Aug 2022.

agencies.name: Frederick-Firestone Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: Took ownership of Tri-Area Ambulance District in 2006 for ALS transport.

agencies.name: Windsor Severance Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: Provides fire and EMS services. Transport capability needs detailed verification.

agencies.name: Mile High Ambulance
agencies.notes: Service area includes Fort Collins, Greeley, Loveland, Longmont, Boulder.

agencies.name: Morgan County Ambulance Service
coverage: Parts of Weld, Logan, Washington, Adams counties
agencies.notes: Est. 1967. 1,296 sq mi + 1,300 sq mi mutual aid. 7 ambulances. 2 ALS crews 24/7 (Fort Morgan + Brush). 11 calls/day avg.

agencies.name: Sterling Fire Department / Logan County Ambulance
agencies.notes: Sterling FD began operating Logan County Ambulance Service Nov 2011. 650 sq mi. Founded 1884.

agencies.name: Holyoke EMS (Melissa Memorial Hospital)
agencies.notes: Hospital-based ambulance. Formerly shared volunteer service with Haxtun Hospital District.

agencies.name: Haxtun Health EMS
agencies.notes: Hospital-based. Formerly shared volunteer service with Melissa Memorial.

agencies.name: Sedgwick County Ambulance
agencies.notes: County ambulance service.

agencies.name: City of Yuma Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: ALS. ~1,100 sq mi 911 response area. Mutual aid to Wray, Washington, Phillips, Logan counties.

agencies.name: Wray EMS
agencies.notes: ~1,000 sq mi. 500+ calls/yr. 3 ALS ambulances.

agencies.name: Washington County Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: 24/7 ALS. 2 FT paramedics + 3 PT paramedics + 7 PT EMTs + 3 volunteer drivers. Ambulances in Akron(2), Otis(1), Anton(1), Last Chance(1).

agencies.name: Kit Carson County Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: Est. 1965. 1,300 sq mi. 20 volunteers + FT staff. 6 ambulances. 600+ emergency responses/yr.

agencies.name: Limon Ambulance Service
coverage: Parts of Elbert, Kit Carson, Cheyenne, Washington counties
agencies.notes: ALS. Provides mutual aid across 5+ counties in eastern Colorado.

agencies.name: Cheyenne County Ambulance
agencies.notes: County ambulance service. Emergency and non-emergency transport.

agencies.name: Eagle County Paramedic Services
agencies.notes: CAAS accredited (1 of 4 in CO). 1,692 sq mi. 5 stations. Up to 14 ambulances. 6k+ calls/yr. 100 employees (60 paramedics, 21 EMTs).

agencies.name: Summit Fire & EMS
agencies.notes: Merged Summit County Ambulance Service with Summit Fire & EMS July 2019. 911 response + critical care IFT for all of Summit County.

agencies.name: Aspen Ambulance District
agencies.notes: 32 staff. 24-hour paramedic response and critical care IFT.

agencies.name: Chaffee County EMS
agencies.notes: CM-RETAC member. County-operated EMS.

agencies.name: St. Vincent Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: 24/7/365 in 400 sq mi Lake County. Works with Leadville Lake County Fire Rescue.

agencies.name: South Park Ambulance District
agencies.notes: Est. 1992. ~1,500 sq mi of Park County.

agencies.name: Ute Pass Regional Health Service District
coverage: Parts of Teller, Douglas, Park counties
agencies.notes: 539 sq mi. 2 stations (Woodland Park, Florissant). Coordinates with 5 fire agencies.

agencies.name: Grand County EMS
agencies.notes: 1,875 sq mi. 4 stations. 41 FT staff. BLS/ALS/Critical Care. Became full-time paid 2001.

agencies.name: Steamboat Springs Area Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: 380 sq mi, ~8k residents. Fire, EMS, and ambulance services. FF/EMTs with several paramedics.

agencies.name: Craig Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: Moffat County EMS Council agencies include Craig Fire & Rescue, TMH EMS, Maybell EMS, Tri-State EMS.

agencies.name: The Memorial Hospital EMS (TMH EMS)
agencies.notes: Hospital-based EMS in Moffat County. Usually only 1 ambulance crew covering area nearly as large as Connecticut.

agencies.name: Meeker Fire & Rescue (Rio Blanco Fire Protection District)
agencies.notes: Est. 1933. Merged with Meeker Ambulance Service 1997. 1,940 sq mi, ~2,700 people. FT chief + volunteers.

agencies.name: Rangely Hospital District Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: ~2,000 sq mi service area from county line near Dinosaur.

agencies.name: Grand Junction Fire Department
agencies.notes: WestCO dispatch. Ambulance billing through Wittman Enterprises.

agencies.name: Mesa County Fire Authority
agencies.notes: Formed Jan 2022. 221 sq mi rural western Colorado. Fire, rescue, EMS.

agencies.name: Glenwood Springs Fire Department
agencies.notes: All Garfield County fire departments (Carbondale, Glenwood Springs, Rifle, Parachute) running their own 911 ambulance services.

agencies.name: Carbondale & Rural Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: Runs own 911 ambulance service in district.

agencies.name: Rifle Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: Runs own 911 ambulance service in district.

agencies.name: Parachute Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: Runs own 911 ambulance service in district.

agencies.name: Burning Mountains Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: Assuming 911 ambulance service in its district. Property mill levy for fire and ambulance.

agencies.name: Montrose Fire Protection District
agencies.notes: 1,100+ sq mi. Dispatched by WestCO.

agencies.name: Delta County Ambulance District
agencies.notes: Est. 1999 (merger of Cedaredge and Delta County FD1 EMS). 30 FT + 20 PT EMTs/Paramedics. 3 crews from 2 stations 24/7.

agencies.name: North Fork EMS
coverage: Parts of Delta, Gunnison, Montrose counties
agencies.parent_org: North Fork Ambulance Health Service District
agencies.notes: Sole provider for North Fork Valley. Stations in Crawford, Hotchkiss, Paonia. 800+ calls/yr. 1,500 sq mi.

agencies.name: Gunnison Valley Health Paramedics
agencies.notes: CO Ambulance Service of the Year 2020. Hospital-affiliated.

agencies.name: Ouray County EMS
agencies.notes: Volunteers + paid staff. CPR services and training.

agencies.name: Telluride Fire Protection District EMS
agencies.notes: 350 sq mi. 6 ambulances across multiple stations. 24/7/365.

agencies.name: Durango Fire & Rescue
agencies.notes: 9 ALS ambulances (5 staffed 24/7, 3 backup, 1 IFT). Covers City of Durango and La Plata County.

agencies.name: Pagosa Springs Medical Center EMS
agencies.notes: Sole 911 EMS ambulance provider for Archuleta County and portions of Hinsdale and Mineral counties. 24/7.

agencies.name: Silverton Medical Rescue
agencies.notes: One of first combination EMS + Search & Rescue teams.

agencies.name: Montezuma County EMS
agencies.notes: Cortez Fire & Rescue, Mancos Fire & Rescue, Lewis-Arriola Fire & Rescue, Pleasant View Fire & Rescue all provide EMS.

agencies.name: Dove Creek Ambulance District
agencies.notes: Medic 1 & 2. Residents voted on mill levy for ambulance service.

agencies.name: Alamosa County Ambulance
agencies.notes: SLV RETAC member.

agencies.name: Conejos County Ambulance
agencies.notes: ALS county service. ~1,300 sq mi. 8 FT + 8 PT staff. 3 paramedics, 1 CC paramedic, 1 EMT-I, 2 AEMTs, 1 EMT.

agencies.name: Costilla Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: ALS capable but staffing tenuous per SLV RETAC.

agencies.name: Mineral County Ambulance
agencies.notes: SLV RETAC member.

agencies.name: Del Norte Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: Receives supplemental public funding from Rio Grande County Ambulance District.

agencies.name: Monte Vista Community Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: Receives supplemental public funding from Rio Grande County Ambulance District.

agencies.name: Northern Saguache Ambulance District
agencies.notes: SLV RETAC member. Serves areas outside Baca Grande POA.

agencies.name: Spanish Peaks EMS
agencies.notes: 24-hour 911 response. Serving Huerfano County since 1980 (originally Huerfano County EMS, renamed 2019). 1,500 sq mi.

agencies.name: Trinidad Ambulance District
agencies.notes: Southern Colorado RETAC transport agency.

agencies.name: Pueblo Fire Department
agencies.notes: 70%+ of incidents are medical. All engines ALS equipped. All personnel EMT-B minimum. AMR handles primary transport.

agencies.name: Pueblo West Fire Department
agencies.notes: ~50 sq mi plus I-25 corridor.

agencies.name: Custer County EMS
agencies.notes: Southern Colorado RETAC member.

agencies.name: Bent County Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: County ambulance service.

agencies.name: Crowley County Ambulance Department
agencies.notes: Located at 611 E. 9th Street, Ordway.

agencies.name: Kiowa County Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: BLS. ~180 calls/yr. Bulk of calls around Eads.

agencies.name: Lamar Ambulance Service (Lamar Fire Department)
agencies.notes: Founded 1975. ~1,240 sq mi coverage. Part of Lamar Fire Department.

agencies.name: Southeast Colorado Hospital Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: Stationed in Springfield. ~1,900 sq mi. Also provides periodic coverage to edges of Prowers, Las Animas, Bent counties.

agencies.name: La Junta Fire Protection District & EMS
agencies.notes: Southern/Southeastern Colorado RETAC transport agency. AVRMC is level 4 trauma center.

agencies.name: Arkansas Valley Ambulance District
agencies.notes: Listed on 5280fire as Colorado ambulance agency.

agencies.name: Longmont Fire Department (with AMR)
agencies.notes: 93 firefighters + 18 AMR ambulance personnel embedded in fire stations. AMR ambulances at 3 of 6 stations.

agencies.name: Boulder Rural Fire Rescue
agencies.notes: Provides ambulance service in rural Boulder County areas.

agencies.name: Northglenn Ambulance
agencies.notes: Commerce City contract ended May 2022. Current 911 service areas may have changed.

agencies.name: Columbine Ambulance
agencies.notes: Listed on 5280fire as Colorado ambulance agency. Exact 911 service areas need verification.

agencies.name: EMS Unlimited
agencies.notes: Garfield County EMS provider listed by GarcoEMTAC.

agencies.name: Rocky Mountain Mobile Medical
agencies.notes: Locally owned. Serves Pikes Peak Region. Exact 911 vs IFT split needs verification.

agencies.name: Asteri EMS
agencies.notes: Emergency & non-emergency medical transportation. Exact 911 role needs verification.

known_gaps: Jackson County (North Park) - ambulance provider unknown
Hinsdale County - likely covered by Pagosa Springs Medical Center or Lake City volunteer
Many small volunteer fire departments in rural counties that may provide BLS first response but not transport
Exact boundaries between overlapping agencies (e.g., AMR vs fire department primary zones)
Colorado Springs 911 transport status post-April 2025 AMR contract expiration
Several 5280fire listed agencies not yet researched: Ambulnz Medical, Colorado Motocross Medics, Children's One
Weld County has many fire districts - not all transport capabilities verified
El Paso County has 15+ fire districts - only Security FPD confirmed as transport
Some agencies listed may primarily do IFT rather than 911
